---
title: "20260415_CRC_metabolite_test"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-15"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)


library(data.table)
library(purrr)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(broom)

#we are using git remote set-url origin git@github.com:USERNAME/REPOSITORY.git
#to get git to use ssh key?
```

```{r handling function}
parse_mz_rt_exact <- function(exposure) {
  exposure_core <- exposure |>
    str_remove("^[^_]+_[^_]+__") |>
    str_remove("__dup\\d+$")

  ends_mz_n <- str_detect(exposure_core, "(m/z|n)$")

  nums_list <- str_extract_all(exposure_core, "\\d+\\.?\\d*") |>
    map(as.numeric)

  mz <- map2_dbl(nums_list, ends_mz_n, function(nums, end_flag) {
    if (length(nums) == 0) return(NA_real_)

    if (end_flag) {
      return(dplyr::last(nums))
    }

    if (length(nums) >= 2) {
      first_num <- nums[1]
      second_num <- nums[2]

      if (!is.na(first_num) && first_num < 20 && second_num >= 20) {
        return(second_num)
      }

      return(first_num)
    }

    nums[1]
  })

  rt <- map2_dbl(nums_list, ends_mz_n, function(nums, end_flag) {
    if (length(nums) == 0) return(NA_real_)

    if (end_flag) {
      return(if (length(nums) >= 2) nums[1] else NA_real_)
    }

    if (length(nums) >= 2) {
      first_num <- nums[1]
      second_num <- nums[2]

      if (!is.na(first_num) && first_num < 20 && second_num >= 20) {
        return(first_num)
      }

      return(second_num)
    }

    NA_real_
  })

  tibble(mz = mz, rt = rt)
}
```

# Import Ancillary Data
```{r}
ancillary_meta_data <- readRDS("../data/ancillary_meta_data.RDS")
```


# Import metabolite data
```{r data}
date()

ccreoh <- fread("../../Can_we_see_it/CCREOH_ST002118_AN003466_Analytical_Dataset.csv")

# 1. Pivot the metabolite columns into a long format
ccreoh_long <- ccreoh |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("ST002118"), # Selects all columns starting with your study ID
    names_to = "exposure",          # Moves column names into a new column called "exposure"
    values_to = "intensity"         # Moves the actual numeric data into "intensity"
  )

# 2. Use the handling function (D LLoyd) to extract m/z and RT
# We use mutate + unpack to keep everything aligned in one data frame
ccreoh_cleaned <- ccreoh_long |>
  mutate(parsed = parse_mz_rt_exact(exposure)) |>
  unpack(parsed)

#remove columns with NA's
empty_cols <- ccreoh_cleaned |>
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ all(is.na(.)))) |>
  pivot_longer(everything()) |>
  filter(value == TRUE) |>
  pull(name)

#what will be dropped
print(empty_cols)

ccreoh_cleaned <- ccreoh_cleaned |>
  select(where(~ !all(is.na(.))))

head(ccreoh_cleaned)

# Pull out metadata
ccreoh_sample_table <- ccreoh_cleaned |>
  dplyr::select(-intensity,
                -exposure,
                -mz,
                -rt) |>
  dplyr::distinct()

#example, pull out a few peaks:
#0.41_113.0209 from everyone
ccreoh_cleaned |>
  dplyr::filter(mz == 113.0209 & rt == 0.41)

ccreoh_cleaned |>
  dplyr::filter(mz == 113.0209 & rt == 0.41) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = intensity)) +
  geom_histogram() +
  theme_minimal() +
  ggtitle("0.41_113.0209")

#ST002118_AN003466__0.72_117.0738m
ccreoh_cleaned |>
  dplyr::filter(mz == 117.0738 & rt == 0.72)

ccreoh_cleaned |>
  dplyr::filter(mz == 117.0738 & rt == 0.72) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = intensity)) +
  geom_histogram() +
  theme_minimal() +
  ggtitle("0.72_117.0738m/z")
date()
```

#Generate Fake Ancillary Metabolite Data
```{r}
#pick 1,000 peaks
set.seed(1212) # For reproducibility
sample_peaks <- ccreoh_cleaned |>
  dplyr::select(exposure, mz, rt) |>
  dplyr::filter(!grepl("n", exposure)) |>
  dplyr::distinct() |>
  dplyr::slice_sample(n=1000)

sample_table <- ancillary_meta_data |>
  dplyr::mutate(pid = person_id,
                year = str_extract(observation_datetime_utc, "^\\d{4}")) |>
  dplyr::select(pid, year, sex, self_report)

```

```{r}
# 1. Prepare the reference parameters from ccreoh_cleaned
# measure the parameters of each peak based on real observations
peak_logic <- ccreoh_cleaned |>
  filter(exposure %in% sample_peaks$exposure) |>
  group_by(exposure) |>
  summarise(
    # Calculate the proportion of values that are ZERO
    prop_zero = sum(intensity == 0, na.rm = TRUE) / n(),
    # Distribution stats (only for the actual signals > 0)
    log_mean = mean(log(intensity[intensity > 0]), na.rm = TRUE),
    log_sd   = sd(log(intensity[intensity > 0]), na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups  = "drop")

set.seed(1212) # For reproducibility
dummy_peak_file <- sample_table |>
  select(pid) |>
  cross_join(peak_logic) |>
  # Randomly decide if this person gets a 0 based on that peak's zero-rate
  mutate(is_zero = rbinom(n(), 1, prop_zero),
    # If is_zero is 1, return 0. Otherwise, pull from log-normal.
    fake_intensity = if_else(is_zero == 1, 0, exp(rnorm(n(), mean = log_mean, sd = log_sd)))) |>
  select(pid, exposure, intensity = fake_intensity)


# Compare means of non-NA values
stats_compare <- bind_rows(ccreoh_cleaned |> 
                             filter(exposure %in% sample_peaks$exposure) |>
                             mutate(source = "Real"),
                           dummy_peak_file |>
                             mutate(source = "Dummy")) |>
  group_by(exposure, source) |>
  summarise(mean_int = mean(intensity, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = source, values_from = mean_int)

ggplot(stats_compare, aes(x = Real, y = Dummy)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, color = "darkgreen") +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  scale_x_log10() + scale_y_log10() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Correlation between Peak Means (Log-Normal Fix)")

zero_compare <- bind_rows(
  ccreoh_cleaned |> filter(exposure %in% sample_peaks$exposure) |> mutate(source = "Real"),
  dummy_peak_file |> mutate(source = "Dummy")
) |>
  group_by(exposure, source) |>
  summarise(zero_count = sum(intensity == 0), .groups = "drop") |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = source, values_from = zero_count) |>
  dplyr::mutate(Dummy_Per_Sample = Dummy / dim(ancillary_meta_data)[1],
                Real_Per_Sample = Real / dim(ccreoh)[1])

ggplot(zero_compare, aes(x = Real_Per_Sample,
                         y = Dummy_Per_Sample)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, color = "orange") +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Count of Zero Values (0) per Peak",
       x = "Actual Zeros per Sample", y = "Synthetic Zeros per Sample")

dummy_peak_file_wide <- dummy_peak_file |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = exposure, values_from = intensity)

# Tag the data sources and combine
comparison_df <- bind_rows(
  ccreoh_cleaned |>
    filter(exposure %in% sample_peaks$exposure) |>
    select(exposure, intensity) |>
    mutate(source = "Real"),
    
  dummy_peak_file |>
    select(exposure, intensity) |>
    mutate(source = "Dummy")
)

ggplot(comparison_df, aes(x = intensity, fill = source)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_x_log10() + # Metabolomics data is almost always better viewed on log scale
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Global Distribution: Real vs. Dummy Data",
       x = "Intensity (Log10 Scale)",
       y = "Density")

# 1. Capture the constant sample size
target_n <- dim(ccreoh)[1]

# 2. Downsample the dummy data
dummy_downsampled <- dummy_peak_file |>
  group_by(exposure) |>
  # Now that target_n is a single number (a constant), this works perfectly
  slice_sample(n = target_n) |>
  ungroup()

# 3. Combine and Plot
comparison_df <- bind_rows(
  ccreoh_cleaned |> 
    filter(exposure %in% sample_peaks$exposure) |> 
    mutate(source = "Real"),
    
  dummy_downsampled |> 
    mutate(source = "Dummy")
)

# 1. Pick 9 random exposure names from your list
set.seed(1212) # So you see the same 9 peaks every time you run this
subset_peaks <- sample(unique(comparison_df$exposure), 9)

# 2. Filter and Plot
comparison_df |>
  filter(exposure %in% subset_peaks) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = intensity, fill = source)) +
  geom_histogram(alpha = 0.5, position = "identity", bins = 30) +
  scale_x_log10() + 
  facet_wrap(~exposure, scales = "free", ncol = 3) + # Forces a nice 3x3 grid
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 7)) + # Shrink titles so they fit
  labs(title = "Detailed Check: Real vs. Dummy (Random 9 Peaks)",
       subtitle = "Comparing shapes on a log10 scale",
       x = "Intensity", y = "Count")
```
Save off for future use
```{r}
saveRDS(dummy_peak_file,
        file = "../../Ancillary_pid_1000DUMMY_peak_values.RDS")

#system("gsutil cp ../../Ancillary_pid_1000DUMMY_peak_values.RDS gc_bucket_location")
system("gsutil cp ../../Ancillary_pid_1000DUMMY_peak_values.RDS gs://ancillarydemographics-wb-mighty-apple-994/")
system("gsutil cp ../../Ancillary_pid_1000DUMMY_peak_values.RDS gs://can-we-see-it-wb-mighty-apple-994")

```

```{r}
dummy1 <- readRDS(file = "../../Can_we_see_it/Ancillary_pid_1000DUMMY_peak_values.RDS")

list.files("/home/rstudio/workspace/Can_we_see_it/")
```

process the data
```{r}
# 1. Start with the dummy data
dummy_processed <- dummy1 |>
  
  # --- STEP 1: FILTERING (Feature-wise) ---
  # Remove peaks that are "zero" in more than 20% of samples.
  # This prevents noise from being treated as signal.
  group_by(exposure) |>
  filter(sum(intensity == 0) / n() < 0.20) |>
  ungroup() |>
  
  # --- STEP 2: NORMALIZATION (Sample-wise) ---
  # Corrects for the fact that some people have "brighter" samples than others.
  # We divide every peak in a person's profile by that person's median intensity.
  group_by(pid) |>
  mutate(intensity = intensity / median(intensity[intensity > 0], na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  ungroup() |>
  
  # --- STEP 3: TRANSFORMATION (Log) ---
  # Pulls in the "long tail" of high-intensity values.
  # We use log1p (log(x + 1)) to keep zeros as zeros.
  mutate(intensity = log1p(intensity)) |>
  
  # --- STEP 4: SCALING (Feature-wise / Autoscaling) ---
  # Subtract the mean and divide by SD for each peak.
  # This makes every peak have a Mean of 0 and an SD of 1.
  group_by(exposure) |>
  mutate(intensity = (intensity - mean(intensity, na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(intensity, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  ungroup()

# Quick check: The mean for any peak should now be ~0 and SD ~1
summary(dummy_processed$intensity)
```


check Sex to see if there are significant peaks
```{r}
# Join with sex/year/self_report and run the model
final_results <- dummy_processed %>%
  inner_join(sample_table, by = "pid") %>%
  filter(sex %in% c("Male", "Female")) %>%
  group_by(exposure) %>%
  do(tidy(lm(intensity ~ sex + year + self_report, data = .))) %>%
  filter(term == "sexMale") %>%
  mutate(p_adj = p.adjust(p.value, method = "BH"))

# Check your top hits (likely empty, which is the goal for dummy data!)
print(head(final_results))

#p-value histogram
ggplot(data = final_results,
       aes(x = p.value)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 100)

mean(final_results$p.value < 0.05)
```
